title: Add spec.resources field to Keystone CRD with defaults and validation
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#120](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/120)

Add an optional `spec.resources` field of type `*corev1.ResourceRequirements` to `KeystoneSpec` in `keystone_types.go` with `+optional` marker and `omitempty` JSON tag. In the defaulting webhook (`Default()` in `keystone_webhook.go`), inject sensible defaults (256Mi/512Mi memory, 100m/500m CPU) when `spec.Resources == nil`. In the validation webhook (`validateKeystone()`), reject specs where any resource request exceeds its corresponding limit via `specPath.Child("resources")` accumulated into `field.ErrorList`. Wire `spec.Resources` into the `keystone-api` container in `buildKeystoneDeployment()` in `reconcile_deployment.go`. Regenerate the CRD manifest via `controller-gen`. Add unit tests for webhook defaulting (nil → defaults, explicit preserved), validation (valid, request > limit rejected), and deployment builder (resources in container spec). Update CRD reference docs at `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`.

**Rationale:** Without resource requests, pods run as BestEffort QoS and the existing HPA (CC-0038) cannot compute utilization ratios since it targets CPU/memory utilization percentages against undefined baselines. This is a correctness bug — HPA is effectively non-functional without defined resource requests to compute percentages against.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go
- helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml
- docs/reference/keystone-crd.md
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical

## Summary

Add an optional `spec.resources` field of type `*corev1.ResourceRequirements` to the Keystone CRD. The defaulting webhook injects sensible defaults (256Mi/512Mi memory, 100m/500m CPU) when the field is nil, and `buildKeystoneDeployment` wires the value into the container spec. This fixes a correctness gap where HPA (CC-0038) targets utilization percentages against undefined baselines, and ensures pods are scheduled with Burstable QoS class.

## Scope

**Included:**
- `spec.resources` field on `KeystoneSpec` as `*corev1.ResourceRequirements` with `+optional` marker and `omitempty` JSON tag — follows the existing pointer-field pattern used by `Autoscaling`, `NetworkPolicy`, `Federation`
- Defaulting webhook (`Default()`) sets defaults (256Mi request / 512Mi limit memory, 100m request / 500m limit CPU) when `spec.Resources == nil` — unconditionally, regardless of autoscaling config
- Validation webhook (`validateKeystone()`) rejects specs where any resource request exceeds its corresponding limit, accumulating errors into `field.ErrorList` per existing pattern
- `buildKeystoneDeployment()` applies `spec.Resources` to the `keystone-api` container (~single line addition at container struct)
- Regenerated CRD manifest in `helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` via `controller-gen`
- Unit tests: webhook defaulting (nil → defaults, explicit values preserved), validation (valid, request > limit rejected), deployment builder (resources appear in container spec for both default and custom values)
- CRD reference docs update (`docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`)

**Excluded:**
- Separate resource fields for sidecar/init containers (YAGNI — only `keystone-api` container exists)
- VPA integration (separate concern, not requested)
- Resource quota or LimitRange enforcement (cluster-level concern, not operator's responsibility)
- Helm `values.yaml` changes for resource defaults (CRD webhook handles defaults; operator pod resources are a separate concern)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph CRD["KeystoneSpec"]
        RES["spec.resources\n*corev1.ResourceRequirements"]
    end

    subgraph Webhook["Defaulting + Validation Webhook"]
        DEF["Default: inject 256Mi/512Mi mem\n100m/500m CPU when nil"]
        VAL["Validate: requests <= limits"]
    end

    subgraph Reconciler["reconcile_deployment.go"]
        BUILD["buildKeystoneDeployment"]
        CONTAINER["Container.Resources = spec.resources"]
    end

    subgraph K8s["Kubernetes"]
        SCHED["Scheduler: QoS-aware placement"]
        HPA["HPA: utilization ratio\ncpu_used / cpu_request"]
    end

    RES --> DEF --> VAL --> BUILD --> CONTAINER
    CONTAINER --> SCHED
    CONTAINER --> HPA
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant WH as Webhook
    participant R as Reconciler
    participant K as Kubernetes

    U->>WH: Create/Update Keystone CR
    alt spec.resources is nil
        WH->>WH: Inject default resources
    end
    WH->>WH: Validate requests <= limits
    WH-->>R: Admitted CR
    R->>R: buildKeystoneDeployment with spec.resources
    R->>K: Apply Deployment
    K->>K: Schedule pod with QoS class
    K->>K: HPA computes utilization ratios
```

## Key Components

- **`KeystoneSpec.Resources`** (`keystone_types.go`): New `*corev1.ResourceRequirements` pointer field with `// +optional` marker and `json:"resources,omitempty"` tag — follows the same pattern as `Autoscaling *AutoscalingSpec`, `NetworkPolicy *NetworkPolicySpec`
- **Defaulting webhook** (`keystone_webhook.go` `Default()`): Add `if obj.Spec.Resources == nil { obj.Spec.Resources = &corev1.ResourceRequirements{...} }` — mirrors the existing `if obj.Spec.Replicas == 0` pattern but with nil-check for pointer type
- **Validation webhook** (`keystone_webhook.go` `validateKeystone()`): Add resource request-vs-limit check under `specPath.Child("resources")`, appending to the existing `field.ErrorList` accumulator. Check each resource type (CPU, memory) independently
- **Deployment builder** (`reconcile_deployment.go` `buildKeystoneDeployment()`): Set `Resources: *keystone.Spec.Resources` on the `keystone-api` container struct literal — single field addition alongside existing `Name`, `Image`, `Command`, `Ports`, `Probes`, `VolumeMounts`
- **CRD manifest** (`helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`): Regenerated via `controller-gen` to include the `resources` field with embedded `corev1.ResourceRequirements` schema
- **Tests**: Webhook tests (`keystone_webhook_test.go`) — default injection when nil, preservation of explicit values, rejection of request > limit; Deployment tests (`reconcile_deployment_test.go`) — resources appear in container spec for both defaulted and custom values
